assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed-cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
clk_div remains defined (no X) and exhibits regular toggling over the observation window
cases.goal: Confirm functional progress of the frequency divider after reset without assuming exact latency.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
Conservative observation due to unknown exact division ratio and timing model.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.
Release rst_n and monitor clk_div for several clock cycles
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe over at least 10 clock cycles to capture multiple periods of clk_div

expected_properties: clk_div toggles at a lower frequency than clk, maintaining a stable period ratio consistent with odd division
No glitches or X-propagation appear on clk_div during observation
cases.goal: Validate that the divided clock exhibits the expected slower, regular toggling behavior without assuming exact cycle counts.
notes: Observation window is conservative due to unknown exact divisor value.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
stimulus_intent: Observe relative frequency of clk_div versus clk over multiple clock cycles
timing_assumptions: Observe over at least 20 clock cycles to capture several periods of clk_div

expected_properties: clk_div returns to a stable, non-X state after each rapid reset release
No metastability or undefined behavior is observed on clk_div during quick reset toggling
cases.goal: Confirm that rapid assert/deassert of the known reset (rst_n) does not corrupt the divider output.
notes: Uses the resolved active-low reset rst_n; clk reset polarity remains ambiguous and is not exercised.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
stimulus_intent: Assert rst_n low, release it, then reassert quickly while clk continues toggling
timing_assumptions: Allow at least 2 clock cycles between reset release and reassert to observe stabilization

unresolved_items: Golden interface described clock 'clk_div', but the RTL port is not a scalar input-like signal.
